task_completion.description: The agent accesses the Emoncms feeds list to retrieve the required data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Emoncms web interface displaying the 'Feeds' page, or a terminal showing an API request/response fetching the feeds.

task_completion.description: The agent creates the specified JSON output file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) or a terminal command creating or writing to '/home/ga/emoncms_status_report.json'.

task_completion.description: The JSON file contains the correct report title and total feeds count.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The visible contents of the JSON file include '"report_title": "Emoncms System Status Report"' and '"total_feeds": 6'.

task_completion.description: The JSON file contains the required keys for each feed object.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The visible contents of the JSON file include a 'feeds' array where objects contain the keys: 'id', 'name', 'tag', 'value', 'unit', and 'engine'.

task_completion.description: The JSON file contains the correct data for all 6 feeds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The visible contents of the JSON file show entries for 'House Power', 'Appliances', 'Solar PV', 'House Temperature', 'Heat Pump Power', and 'Test Feed' with their respective values and tags.

integrity.description: The agent retrieved the feed data from the Emoncms system rather than hallucinating it.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen interacting with the Emoncms web interface or API before writing the JSON file.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file to the exact required path.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file path '/home/ga/emoncms_status_report.json' is clearly visible in the text editor's save prompt, title bar, or terminal command.

integrity.description: The agent formatted the output as valid JSON.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file contents use proper JSON syntax (curly braces, square brackets, quoted keys, commas between elements).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is exactly '/home/ga/emoncms_status_report.json'. The JSON must contain 'report_title', 'total_feeds', and 'feeds' at the root level. The 'feeds' array must contain objects with keys: 'id', 'name', 'tag', 'value', 'unit', and 'engine'. There are exactly 6 feeds in the system: House Power, Appliances, Solar PV, House Temperature, Heat Pump Power, and Test Feed. All feeds use the PHPFina engine (engine=5).
